message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update comment log on bd-24h7 (stabilize frankenengine-engine quality gates
under rch) with 6 new agent work-log entries from NavyGate, SwiftMill, and
JadeCrane documenting compile-blocker patches in parser.rs, parser_arena.rs,
lowering_pipeline.rs, and attested_execution_cell.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

All 7013+ tests pass, cargo check/clippy/fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-24du","title":"[10.19] Define ATC degraded/offline modes and local-first fallback behavior.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.19 — Adversarial Trust Commons Execution Track (9M)

Why This Exists:
ATC federated intelligence track for privacy-preserving cross-deployment threat learning, robust aggregation, and verifier-backed trust metrics.

Task Objective:
Define ATC degraded/offline modes and local-first fallback behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Federation outage or partition triggers deterministic fallback policy; local risk controls remain functional; rejoin/reconciliation is audited.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/atc_degraded_mode.md`, `tests/integration/atc_partition_fallback.rs`, `artifacts/10.19/atc_degraded_mode_events.jsonl`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-24du/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-24du/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.19] Define ATC degraded/offline modes and local-first fallback behavior.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.19] Define ATC degraded/offline modes and local-first fallback behavior." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.19] Define ATC degraded/offline modes and local-first fallback behavior." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.19] Define ATC degraded/offline modes and local-first fallback behavior." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.19] Define ATC degraded/offline modes and local-first fallback behavior." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Federation outage or partition triggers deterministic fallback policy; local risk controls remain functional; rejoin/reconciliation is audited.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkLantern","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:06.252412913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T05:03:43.782954103Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T05:03:43.782925760Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-19","self-contained","test-obligations"]}
-{"id":"bd-24h7","title":"Stabilize frankenengine-engine all-target quality gates under rch (warning debt + timeout)","description":"rch remote all-target checks for frankenengine-engine from clean /data/projects snapshot hit broad warning output across many tests/src files and eventually timeout (exit 137), preventing deterministic clippy/check closure. Capture and resolve warning-heavy surfaces (unused imports/vars, dead code) or adjust gate strategy so cargo check --all-targets and cargo clippy --all-targets can complete reliably under remote timeout budgets.","notes":"2026-03-02 warning-reduction pass #1 applied (semantics-preserving test assertions only) in:
- crates/franken-engine/src/control_plane_benchmark_split_gate.rs
- crates/franken-engine/src/demo_claim_linkage_gate.rs
- crates/franken-engine/src/engine_object_id.rs
- crates/franken-engine/src/eprocess_guardrail.rs
- crates/franken-engine/src/evidence_ordering.rs
- crates/franken-engine/src/flow_envelope.rs
- crates/franken-engine/src/northstar_scorecard.rs
- crates/franken-engine/src/optimization_baseline.rs

Each change reads/asserts the mutated clone value to eliminate dead-store warnings (`value assigned ... never read`) without behavior changes.

Validation status:
- Remote `cargo check -p frankenengine-engine` previously passes (exit=0) on clean snapshot lane.
- Re-validation runs continue to suffer from rch execute->heartbeat stale in this shared environment; explicit cancel used when stale to avoid local fallback.

2026-03-03 GoldLantern support pass:
- Reproduced timeout via rch: cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --all-targets -> exit 137 at ~302s.
- Applied warning cleanup in franken_engine files: budgeted_optimization.rs, static_analysis_graph_integration.rs, expected_loss_selector_enrichment_integration.rs.
- Follow-up rch validation (--lib --tests) blocked by unrelated compile errors in ast/lowering_pipeline/parser_arena/static_semantics currently present in active branch state.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RainyWolf","created_at":"2026-03-02T20:39:22.261019247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T04:45:46.571121403Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":27,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"FrostyWaterfall","text":"Applied warning-reduction edits in crates/franken-engine/src/policy_checkpoint.rs and crates/franken-engine/src/proof_schema.rs by asserting mutated clone values in clone-independence tests (semantics-preserving). Offloaded validation run via rch command: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --lib policy_head_clone_independence -- --exact. Current blocker: compile exits early due to unrelated unclosed delimiter in crates/franken-engine/src/static_semantics.rs (diagnostics around lines ~860/903/974, reported at line 4530), so warning confirmation for this slice is temporarily blocked.","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:14:14Z"},{"id":28,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"FrostyWaterfall","text":"Validation follow-up: local rustfmt parse check on crates/franken-engine/src/static_semantics.rs passes, but offloaded rch runs are nondeterministic (one worker reports unclosed-delimiter parse error in static_semantics.rs; another worker hangs/silences after reaching frankenengine-engine compile). Post-change warning confirmation for this slice remains blocked by upstream compile/worker instability.","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:21:39Z"},{"id":29,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"FrostyWaterfall","text":"Landed additional warning-reduction patch in crates/franken-engine/src/parser_error_recovery.rs: added read-back assertions after three mutated  assignments in clone-independence tests (lines around 1822/1830/1838 warning sites). File passes rustfmt --check. Existing rch warning output for this file came from a run started before this patch.","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:27:54Z"},{"id":30,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"FrostyWaterfall","text":"Correction to prior comment: parser_error_recovery patch asserts mutated original values (not clone) after assignment in three clone-independence tests, specifically to clear unused_assignments warnings at the historical 1822/1830/1838 sites.","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:27:59Z"},{"id":31,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"FrostyWaterfall","text":"Consolidated validation outcomes after my 3-file warning-reduction patches: (1) rch cargo test --lib policy_head_clone_independence run ended exit=101 after ~556s with broad AST migration errors (E0560/E0308/E0615) across ast.rs/parser.rs/static_semantics.rs; (2) rch cargo check --tests run ended exit=137 after ~302s with parser.rs method-vs-field and BindingPattern mismatch errors before timeout. Reservations released; bead remains blocked on upstream parser/AST migration + timeout pressure.","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:35:24Z"},{"id":32,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"SunnyBridge","text":"Ran remote validation via `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_sunnybridge cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --all-targets` from `/data/projects/franken_engine`.

Observed concrete compile blocker:
- `error[E0689]` in `crates/franken-engine/src/runtime_diagnostics_cli.rs:1318`
- Failing expression: `baseline_risk_millionths.saturating_add(signal_risk_millionths)`
- Root cause: `baseline_risk_millionths` inferred as ambiguous default integer while `signal_risk_millionths` is `u64`.

Minimal corrective patch:
```rust
let baseline_risk_millionths: u64 = match input.preflight.verdict {
    PreflightVerdict::Green => 100_000,
    PreflightVerdict::Yellow => 400_000,
    PreflightVerdict::Red => 700_000,
};
```

Coordination note:
- I attempted to reserve this file in `/data/projects/franken_engine` via Agent Mail, but it is exclusively reserved by `BlackCat`, so I did not edit to avoid overlap.
- Shared this failure + patch suggestion with both `RainyWolf` (franken_node thread `br-24h7`) and `BlackCat` (franken_engine thread `bd-24h7`).

Operational note:
- The remote build job was cancelled after error capture to avoid holding worker capacity (`rch cancel 29737366419669107`).

Focused rch lib run (session 2673) failed with E0689 at crates/franken-engine/src/runtime_diagnostics_cli.rs:1318 (ambiguous integer type on saturating_add chain). Applied fix in runtime_diagnostics_cli.rs by typing baseline_risk_millionths as u64 with u64 literals. Follow-up rch lib validation run (build 29737366419669120/session 1658) entered stale execute; cancelled explicitly via rch cancel 29737366419669120 to free slots. Current next step: continue validation on narrower targets while queue pressure is high.","created_at":"2026-03-03T03:43:35Z"},{"id":36,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"RainyWolf","text":"Follow-up validation evidence from rch daemon history: build 29737366419669125 (cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --bin runtime_diagnostics --test runtime_diagnostics_cli) completed with exit_code=0 at 2026-03-03T03:46:04Z (duration ~346s). This indicates runtime_diagnostics E0689 surface is no longer blocking on current head. Remaining issue for this bead appears to be all-target/clippy lane stability under concurrent queue pressure (e.g., build 29737366419669104 exit=137 timeout).

Captured deterministic compile blockers before cancellation: E0428 duplicate test function definitions in crates/franken-engine/src/sorted_multisig.rs (multisig_error_display_all_variants at lines 1343 and 1679; multisig_event_type_serde_all_variants at lines 1101 and 1746). Queue remained saturated/stale after error emission, so I cancelled build #29737366419669135 to free slots.","created_at":"2026-03-03T03:51:52Z"},{"id":39,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Follow-up analysis of build #29737366419669135: local source check currently reports single definitions for the two test names flagged by E0428 in sorted_multisig.rs (no duplicate fn names now), so that compiler output likely came from stale remote snapshot state during worker sync churn. Same run showed repeated repo_updater sync-apply pull failures before execute. Suggest treating duplicate-name error as transient snapshot skew unless reproduced again on a clean worker lane.","created_at":"2026-03-03T03:53:11Z"},{"id":40,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"BlackHawk","text":"2026-03-03 BlackHawk: taking a support slice on bd-24h7. Plan is to reproduce current frankenengine all-target failure surfaces via rch, reserve only touched files in /data/projects/franken_engine, and land one narrow blocker fix with concrete build/session evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:14:16Z"},{"id":41,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"BlackHawk","text":"2026-03-03 BlackHawk support findings (rch evidence):\n- Offloaded run: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_blackhawk cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --all-targets\n- Queue build id: 29737366419669160 (worker vmi1153651). Captured E0428 duplicate test-name diagnostics in crates/franken-engine/src/dp_budget_accountant.rs (epoch_budget_serde_roundtrip, budget_forecast_serde_roundtrip, accountant_error_implements_std_error) before run went stale; explicitly cancelled build 29737366419669160.\n- Local source in /data/projects/franken_engine no longer reproduces those duplicate symbols: direct grep/awk duplicate-test-name scans across crates/franken-engine/src + tests report zero collisions; dp_budget_accountant currently has renamed variants (e.g., *_small_budget_profile, *_exhausted_profile, *_single_variant).\n- Follow-up offloaded probes (builds 29737366419669163 and 29737366419669168) reached frankenengine-engine and then produced no further diagnostics (stale execute pattern), so both were cancelled to free slots.\nConclusion: current blocker appears to be remote snapshot/worker staleness + queue churn rather than a persistent local duplicate-symbol defect in dp_budget_accountant.","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:21:54Z"},{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"BlackHawk","text":"Additional support evidence (2026-03-03): offloaded narrow lane also stalls without diagnostics.\n- Command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_blackhawk4 cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --lib\n- Queue build id: 29737366419669175 (worker vmi1153651).\n- Behavior: progressed through dependency compilation and reached , then no further compiler output (same stale execute pattern).\n- Action: cancelled build 29737366419669175 to free capacity.\n\nInterpretation: failure mode is currently operational (stale remote execution/snapshot churn), not limited to all-target breadth. Practical lane strategy should include explicit stale detection + cancellation and potentially worker/snapshot refresh before treating emitted compile errors as definitive.","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:26:23Z"},{"id":43,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"BlackHawk","text":"Correction to prior note: the observed stall point was at the compiler stage where cargo reports frankenengine-engine is being checked.\n\nRe-stated evidence:\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_blackhawk4 cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --lib\n- Build id 29737366419669175 (vmi1153651)\n- Reached crate-check phase, then emitted no additional diagnostics (stale execute pattern)\n- Build cancelled intentionally to free queue slots","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:26:29Z"},{"id":44,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"DustyLake","text":"2026-03-03 DustyLake support run (read-only, no edits): offloaded  from /data/projects/franken_engine. Build id 29737366419669176 on worker vmi1153651. Pipeline notes: repo_updater sync-apply failed 3/3 for asupersync/franken_engine pull, then transfer sync fallback succeeded (24s sync up) and compile reached frankenengine-engine. After that, no new compiler diagnostics were emitted while heartbeat stayed alive in execute phase; rch status showed progress_age increasing to 191s at build_age 309s. To avoid slot starvation, I cancelled explicitly: Error: Build not found or already completed -> completed cleanly; final result exit_code=130, duration=318837ms. This reproduces queue/execution staleness behavior rather than a deterministic new Rust diagnostic.","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:31:12Z"},{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"DustyLake","text":"Correction to comment #44: command was rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_dustylake cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --all-targets (no edits). Build 29737366419669176 reached frankenengine-engine, then produced no further diagnostics while heartbeat stayed alive in execute. rch status showed progress_age_secs=191 at detector_build_age_secs=309; I then ran rch cancel 29737366419669176 and cancellation completed cleanly (exit_code=130, duration_ms=318837 in history). Separate accidental re-run build 29737366419669181 was also cancelled immediately to free slots.","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:31:20Z"},{"id":46,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"BronzeRiver","text":"Support slice (BronzeRiver): reproduced deterministic compile blockers via rch all-target run from /data/projects/franken_engine (build 29737366419669161, exit 101). Errors observed: duplicate tests in crates/franken-engine/src/dp_budget_accountant.rs (E0428) and CellError constructor mismatch in crates/franken-engine/src/cancellation_lifecycle.rs (E0063/E0559 family in stale snapshots). Coordinated to avoid overlap with CrimsonBarn on dp_budget_accountant; took cancellation_lifecycle only. Applied targeted fix in cancellation_lifecycle.rs: added required error_code field in CellError::CxThreading test initializer. Validation attempts were all offloaded via rch, but heavy queue contention/sync churn forced cancellations before deterministic post-fix completion (cancel ops on builds 29737366419669171, 29737366419669177, 29737366419669183).","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:35:02Z"},{"id":47,"issue_id":"bd-24h7","author":"BronzeRiver","text":"2026-03-03 BronzeRiver support slice (targeted warning cleanup): removed unused imports only in 3 integration tests under /data/projects/franken_engine: session_hostcall_channel_enrichment_integration.rs (dropped ChannelPayload, SessionChannelEvent), global_coherence_checker_integration.rs (dropped BTreeMap), slot_registry_enrichment_integration.rs (dropped PromotionTransition, SlotEntry). Validation attempt was offloaded via rch: build 29737366419669191 running cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --test session_hostcall_channel_enrichment_integration --message-format short (worker vmi1156319). Build entered stale execute/no-progress pattern after reaching frankenengine-engine crate-check phase; cancelled cleanly via Error: Build not found or already completed to free queue capacity. No semantic logic changes in this slice; import-prune only.","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:45:46Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-24k","title":"[10.11] Implement bounded masking helper for tiny atomic product operations.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md — Section 10.11 (FrankenSQLite-Inspired Runtime Systems), cross-ref Section 9G.1

## Why This Exists

Many product operations in franken_node are tiny and atomic — checking a single capability flag, reading a single trust artifact field, incrementing a monotonic counter, toggling a feature gate. These operations are too small to justify the overhead of a full saga or two-phase channel, but they still need to be safe: they must not be interrupted by cancellation mid-write (leaving partial state), they must respect capability context (Cx-first), and they must produce deterministic results. Enhancement Map 9G.1 (capability-context-first product runtime APIs) implies that even the smallest operations must flow through the capability system. The "bounded masking" pattern from FrankenSQLite provides a lightweight primitive for this: a short critical section where cancellation signals are temporarily masked (not ignored — deferred) while the tiny atomic operation completes, with a strict bound on how long masking can last.

This bead implements the bounded masking helper as a product-layer utility, ensuring that tiny atomic operations can complete without cancellation-induced partial writes while maintaining the overall cancellation discipline required by the three-kernel architecture.

## What This Must Do

1. Implement a `bounded_mask<T, F>(max_duration: Duration, op: F) -> Result<T, MaskError>` helper where `op` is a synchronous or near-synchronous closure that executes with cancellation signals deferred (masked).
2. Enforce a strict upper bound on mask duration: if `op` exceeds `max_duration`, the mask is forcibly lifted and the operation is aborted with a `MaskTimeoutExceeded` error. The bound must be configurable but default to a conservative value (e.g., 1ms).
3. Ensure the masking helper requires a valid `CapabilityContext` — it is not callable from ambient (uncapabilitated) code paths. The Cx token is threaded through and available inside the masked closure.
4. After the masked operation completes (or is aborted), immediately deliver any deferred cancellation signals so that the broader workflow can proceed with its cancel-drain-finalize sequence.
5. Emit a structured log event for every mask invocation, including: mask duration, whether the operation completed within the bound, and whether deferred cancellation signals were pending.
6. Provide compile-time or lint-time enforcement that `bounded_mask` closures do not contain `.await` points (masking async operations would defeat the purpose and risk unbounded masking).

## Context from Enhancement Maps

- 9G.1: "Capability-context-first product runtime APIs" — even tiny operations must flow through the capability system.
- 9G.2: "Cancellation as a strict protocol for all long-running orchestration tasks" — bounded masking is the complement: it defines the narrow exception for short operations while preserving overall cancellation discipline.
- 9J.19: "Cancellation-complete protocol discipline with obligation closure proofs" — bounded masking must not violate cancellation completeness; deferred signals must be delivered after unmask.
- Architecture invariant #1 (8.5): Cx-first control — bounded_mask requires CapabilityContext.
- Architecture invariant #3 (8.5): Cancellation protocol semantics — masking is temporary deferral, not suppression.
- Architecture invariant #10 (8.5): No ambient authority — bounded_mask rejects uncapabilitated callers.

## Dependencies

- Upstream: bd-7om (cancel-drain-finalize protocol — bounded masking must interoperate with the cancellation protocol), bd-2g6r (10.15 Cx-first signature policy — provides the CapabilityContext requirement pattern)
- Downstream: bd-93k (checkpoint placement uses bounded_mask for atomic checkpoint writes), bd-3vm (ambient-authority audit gate verifies bounded_mask is not called from ambient paths), bd-1jpo (10.11 section-wide verification gate)

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `bounded_mask` with a compliant operation (< 1ms) completes successfully and returns the operation result.
2. `bounded_mask` with an operation exceeding `max_duration` returns `MaskTimeoutExceeded` error within 2x the configured bound (to account for scheduling jitter).
3. A cancellation signal arriving during a masked operation is deferred and delivered within 1 event-loop tick after the mask is lifted.
4. Calling `bounded_mask` without a valid `CapabilityContext` produces a compile-time error or a runtime `MissingCapabilityContext` panic.
5. A masked closure containing an `.await` point produces a compile-time error (via procedural macro, `!Send` bound, or equivalent enforcement).
6. Structured log event `bounded_mask.invocation` includes: `mask_duration_us`, `completed_within_bound` (bool), `deferred_cancel_pending` (bool), `trace_id`.
7. Under high-frequency invocation (100,000 mask operations per second), overhead per invocation is < 5 microseconds (measured by benchmark).
8. Verification evidence JSON includes invocations_total, completed_within_bound, mask_timeout_exceeded, deferred_cancels_delivered, and avg_mask_duration_us fields.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: (a) Successful masked operation returns correct value; (b) Timed-out operation returns MaskTimeoutExceeded; (c) Deferred cancel signal is delivered after unmask; (d) Missing CapabilityContext is rejected; (e) Nested bounded_mask calls are handled correctly (inner mask respects outer deadline).
- Integration tests: (a) Bounded mask within a cancel-drain-finalize workflow — verify cancel is deferred during mask and delivered after; (b) Bounded mask used for atomic counter increment under concurrent cancellation; (c) Bounded mask used for atomic trust artifact field update.
- Adversarial tests: (a) Closure that sleeps beyond max_duration — verify timeout enforcement; (b) Concurrent cancellation signals during mask — verify all are delivered after unmask in order; (c) Panic inside masked closure — verify mask is lifted and cancel signals are still delivered; (d) Extremely short max_duration (1us) — verify operation still gets a fair chance.
- Structured logs: Events use stable codes (FN-BM-001 through FN-BM-006), include `trace_id`, `cx_id`, `mask_duration_us`, `outcome`. JSON-formatted.

## Expected Artifacts

- docs/specs/section_10_11/bd-24k_contract.md
- crates/franken-node/src/runtime/bounded_mask.rs (or equivalent module path)
- scripts/check_bounded_masking.py (with --json flag and self_test())
- tests/test_check_bounded_masking.py
- artifacts/section_10_11/bd-24k/verification_evidence.json
- artifacts/section_10_11/bd-24k/verification_summary.md","acceptance_criteria":"AC for bd-24k:
1. A BoundedMask<T> helper type wraps tiny atomic operations (operations completing in bounded constant time, no I/O, no allocation) and suppresses cancellation signals for their duration.
2. The masking window has a compile-time upper bound (MAX_MASK_DURATION_NS constant, default 1 microsecond); any operation exceeding this bound in test mode triggers a MASK_BUDGET_EXCEEDED warning.
3. BoundedMask<T> implements a scoped guard pattern: cancellation tokens are checked before entering the mask and immediately after exiting; cancellation that arrives during the masked window is deferred, not dropped.
4. The mask is NOT nestable: attempting to create a BoundedMask inside an existing BoundedMask panics with MASK_NESTING_VIOLATION to prevent unbounded masking chains.
5. Operations inside a BoundedMask must not perform any async .await, heap allocation, or I/O syscall; a debug-mode assertion verifies no .await points exist within the masked scope (or a lint enforces this).
6. Unit tests verify: (a) cancellation arriving during masked window is deferred and delivered after mask drops, (b) mask nesting panics, (c) operation completing within budget succeeds silently, (d) operation exceeding budget in test mode emits MASK_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, (e) cancellation before mask entry aborts immediately without entering the mask.
7. Structured log events: MASK_ENTER / MASK_EXIT / MASK_BUDGET_EXCEEDED / MASK_NESTING_VIOLATION with operation name and elapsed nanoseconds.","notes":"Plan-space QA addendum (2026-02-20):
1) Preserve full canonical-plan scope; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
2) Completion requires comprehensive verification coverage appropriate to scope: unit tests, integration tests, and E2E scripts/workflows with detailed structured logging (stable event/error codes + trace correlation IDs).
3) Completion requires reproducible evidence artifacts (machine-readable + human-readable) that allow independent replay and root-cause triage.
4) Any implementation PR for this bead must include explicit links to the above test/logging artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaSparrow","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:49.981757845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T00:58:49.183287381Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T00:58:49.183244812Z","close_reason":"Completed bounded masking helper and verifier artifacts; cargo failures are pre-existing workspace blockers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-11","self-contained","test-obligations"]}
 {"id":"bd-24s","title":"[10.13] Implement snapshot policy (`every_updates`, `every_bytes`) and bounded replay targets for connector state.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Implement snapshot policy (`every_updates`, `every_bytes`) and bounded replay targets for connector state.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Replay cost is bounded by configured thresholds; snapshots are validated against chain heads; snapshot policy changes are audited.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/state_snapshot_policy.md`, `tests/perf/state_replay_bound.rs`, `artifacts/10.13/snapshot_policy_benchmark.csv`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-24s/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-24s/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Implement snapshot policy (`every_updates`, `every_bytes`) and bounded replay targets for connector state.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Implement snapshot policy (`every_updates`, `every_bytes`) and bounded replay targets for connector state." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Implement snapshot policy (`every_updates`, `every_bytes`) and bounded replay targets for connector state." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.13] Implement snapshot policy (`every_updates`, `every_bytes`) and bounded replay targets for connector state." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Implement snapshot policy (`every_updates`, `every_bytes`) and bounded replay targets for connector state." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:51.960580991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T11:03:46.573161408Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T11:03:46.573132714Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24s","depends_on_id":"bd-19u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:12.524092544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
